channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Perverse Incentives Can Backfire: Lessons from History and Media
item.description: The article explores the phenomenon known as the 'Cobra Effect', where well-intentioned solutions inadvertently create bigger problems. It recounts the possibly apocryphal tale of the British Raj offe...

item.title: Epanko Graphite Project in Tanzania Secures EIB Technical Support for Mine Expansion and Battery Material Processing
item.description: The Epanko Graphite Project, located in the Mahenge area of Morogoro Region, Tanzania, has successfully signed a technical assistance agreement with the European Investment Bank (EIB) to support its e...

item.title: Local sports captain to return after year-long ACL recovery
item.description: After a challenging year sidelined by an anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) tear, the highly regarded local sports captain is set to make a triumphant return to the field. The injury, which forced the p...

item.title: DSE Reaches Historic Milestone as Market Capitalisation Tops TZS 30 Trillion with Banking Sector Leading
item.description: During Week 6 of 2026, the Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) hit historic highs, with Total Market Capitalisation exceeding TZS 30.8 trillion and Domestic Market Capitalisation passing TZS 21.25 tril...

item.title: San Jose Selected as Fourth Expansion Market for PWHL
item.description: The Premier Women's Hockey League (PWHL) is set to expand its reach by adding San Jose, California, as its fourth and final market in the league's latest expansion phase, according to sources familiar...

item.title: The Introduction of Mini-Skirts in 1965 Vilnius and Their Cultural Impact
item.description: In 1965, the appearance of mini-skirts in Vilnius, Lithuania marked a subtle yet significant cultural shift during the Soviet era. Captured by photographer Marius Baranauskas, these images reveal more...

item.title: Albanese declines to confirm extension of fuel excise cut, highlights fuel supply improvements
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has not confirmed whether the temporary fuel excise cut, which currently reduces fuel prices by roughly 26.3 cents per litre, will be extended past its 30 June deadline...

item.title: Community Rally Supports Teen Recovering from Assault in Red Deer
item.description: A 14-year-old girl, Rylin Brinston, was violently attacked near her school in Red Deer, Alberta, earlier this week, sustaining a concussion, internal bleeding, and potential permanent eye damage. The ...

item.title: Angat Dam water level drops below minimum; irrigation supply cut but Metro Manila safe
item.description: The water level at Angat Dam in Bulacan has dropped to 178.21 meters as of 8 a.m. on May 16, 2026, below the minimum operating level of 180 meters. While the situation is still manageable, the Nationa...

item.title: Magereza Academy Shuts Down After Dorm Fire, Learners Injured
item.description: Magereza Academy, a school hosting over 800 learners, has been closed indefinitely following a suspected arson incident that destroyed a dormitory. The fire broke out on Tuesday night around 8:00pm, a...

item.title: Managing Your Finances During Potential Retrenchment in South Africa
item.description: Facing retrenchment at the age of 50 can be particularly challenging, especially when you still have a mortgage and family responsibilities. The article advises workers in this position to prepare fin...

item.title: How TVC Entertainment’s Quick Kash Game Show Works and What Contestants Can Win
item.description: Quick Kash na new high-energy game show wey dey air for TVC Entertainment every Monday and Friday by 1:30 pm, and e don already start to make strong impact for Nigerian TV space. The show gives contes...

item.title: Eric Trump denies business interests in China and announces lawsuit against MS NOW
item.description: Eric Trump, executive vice president of the Trump Organization and son of former US President Donald Trump, has announced plans to sue MS NOW following a broadcast questioning his motives for accompan...

item.title: NEMA alerts public over website hack, urges caution against fake information
item.description: The National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) don issue warning to Nigerians after hackers break into their official website. According to NEMA, dem don start to dey fix the problem, secure the webs...

item.title: Five Hearty Winter Recipes to Keep You Warm in South Africa
item.description: As the Cape Town region experiences persistent rainfall, Tony Jackman from Daily Maverick presents a selection of comforting winter recipes to keep the chill at bay. These suggestions go beyond typica...

item.title: State Bank finalises new currency notes, seeks cabinet approval
item.description: KARACHI: The Governor of State Bank of Pakistan, Jameel Ahmad, confirmed on Friday that the designs for the new banknotes have been finalised and sent to the federal cabinet for approval. The SBP had ...

item.title: NHL fines Golden Knights and Coach Tortorella for media violations after Game 6
item.description: The Vegas Golden Knights were penalized by the NHL following their series-clinching 5-1 win over the Anaheim Ducks. The team was docked a second-round draft pick and coach John Tortorella was fined $1...

item.title: Tanzania urges banks to expand access as financial sector grows steadily
item.description: The Tanzanian government has expressed satisfaction with the steady growth and stability of the country’s financial sector, urging banks and financial institutions to increase access for Tanzanians wh...

item.title: Senators report pressure and threats amid talks of changing Senate leadership
item.description: Just days after Alan Peter Cayetano assumed the position of Senate President, concerns are emerging over potential attempts to replace him again. According to a report by Ian Cruz on '24 Oras,' Cayeta...

item.title: US and Nigerian Forces Conduct Operation to Kill ISIS Deputy Abu Bilal al-Minuki
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced that Abu Bilal al-Minuki, considered the second highest leader in ISIS worldwide, was killed during a joint operation by American and Nigerian forces. According to ...
